article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
obligation_text: Deployers of an emotion recognition system or a biometric categorisation system shall inform the natural persons exposed thereto of the operation of the system, and shall process the personal data in accordance with Regulations (EU) 2016/679 and (EU) 2018/1725 and Directive (EU) 2016/680.
scoring_text: Knowledge of emotion recognition and biometric categorisation disclosure requirements, including affected person notification procedures and coordination with GDPR personal data processing requirements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of emotion recognition and biometric categorisation disclosure requirements, including affected person notification procedures and coordination with GDPR personal data processing requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 50(3) (obligation) intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.358, cosine: 0.510). Secondary: SP-K-014 (SP, cosine: 0.494), SP-S-007 (SP, cosine: 0.465).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.6/10 (raw 0.358). Shared vocabulary: [data, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [affected, biometric, categorisation, coordination, disclosure, emotion] vs WIDAI terms [access, architectural, classification, controls, design, enforce] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.876, R=0.884). Balanced P/R — the 22-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (87.4%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.510 > STS 0.358 (gap +0.152). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.7%. Shared tokens: [data, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [affected, biometric, categorisation, coordination, disclosure, emotion]. WIDAI-only: [access, architectural, classification, controls, design, enforce].
